channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Tens of thousands attend funeral of late Hezbollah leader Nasrallah 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Tens of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut early Sunday to attend the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike on a southern suburb of the Lebanese capital.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wounded, recovered and back to war. Ukrainian soldiers are returning to battle after amputation
item.description: DONETSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian intelligence soldier doesn’t know how long his clinical death lasted after an explosive detonated beneath him. All Andrii Rubliuk remembers is overwhelming cold, darkness and fear.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel delays release of Palestinian prisoners, citing 'humiliating' handovers of hostages
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel says the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners is delayed “until the release of the next hostages has been assured, and without the humiliating ceremonies” at handovers of Israeli captives in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMED JAHJOUH and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Isolated in 'harsh conditions:' Deportee from US details legal limbo in Panama camp near Darien Gap
item.description: A deportee from the United States detained in a camp in rural Panama, among a hundred who refused to return to their countries, has described waiting in limbo under “harsh conditions” and cut off from access to legal council and other rights.
item.dc:creator: Matias Delacroix And Megan Janetsky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Houthis target US fighter jet, drone with SAM missiles for first time
item.description: Houthi rebels fired air missile at United States military fighter jets. While the missile did not strike the jet, the attempted attack escalated the altercation between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sununu urges Zelensky to 'be careful' not to anger Trump
item.description: Former New Hampshire Gov. Chris Sununu (R) advised Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during a recent interview to “be careful” not to anger President Trump. His warning comes as the two leaders have gone tit-for-tat in recent days after U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Hungarians will decide whether Ukraine can join the European Union, Orbán says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Hungary will be the one to decide whether Ukraine is able to achieve its hopes of joining the European Union in the future, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán said Saturday in an escalation of his adversarial posture toward Hungary's war-ravaged neighbor.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thousands rally in Romania's capital to support far-right Georgescu after annulled presidential race
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Thousands of protesters took to the streets of Romania’s capital on Saturday in a show of support for Călin Georgescu, the far-right populist who emerged as the front-runner in last year’s presidential race that was annulled days before the second-round runoff.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 6 more hostages, signals readiness for next phase of shaky ceasefire
item.description: Hamas on Saturday released six Israeli hostages in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, signaling readiness for the next phase of a shaky ceasefire. Avera Mengistu and Tal Shoham were handed to the Red Cross and eventually to Israel in the southern Gazan city of Rafah.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: A senior Russian official says preparations have begun for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Economy, immigration, Elon Musk at center of German election; conservative candidate favored to win
item.description: Germans go to the polls Sunday. Chancellor Scholz's Social Democrats are likely to lose to the conservative CDU party, as the right-wing Alternative for Germany party is likely to make gains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after a long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition after he suffered a long asthmatic respiratory crisis that required high flows of oxygen.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas releases more hostages in exchange for more than 600 Palestinian prisoners as part of ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas freed more hostages on Saturday in exchange for more than 600 Palestinian prisoners as part of the delicate ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of Shiri Bibas positively identified after eventually being handed over by Hamas
item.description: The body of a woman who was presumed to have been one of four dead hostages Hamas handed over to Israel was positively identified Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu decries release of wrong body as a ceasefire violation. Hamas pledges to investigate
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed revenge Friday for what he described as a “cruel and malicious violation” of the ceasefire agreement after authorities determined that a body released by Hamas was not an Israeli mother of two small boys, as the militant group had prom
item.dc:creator: AREEJ HAZBOUN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thousands rally in Slovakia to mark the 2018 slayings of an investigative journalist and his fiancee
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands rallied all across Slovakia on Friday to mark the seventh anniversary of the slayings of an investigative journalist and his fiancee. The rallies are part of a wave of protests against the pro-Russia policies of populist Prime Minister Robert Fico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis is 'fine,' condition not life-threatening, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis "is fine" as his medical condition is not life-threatening, but he is not being released from the hospital yet, this doctors in Italy announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Zelensky 'has no cards,' 'shouldn't be at meetings' with Russia
item.description: President Trump lashed out at Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday, saying he did not think it was important for Zelensky to attend negotiations about the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US not co-sponsoring UN resolution supporting Ukraine: Report
item.description: The U.S. is reportedly refusing to co-sponsor a draft United Nations resolution marking three years since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, signaling weakening U.S. support for Ukraine as President Trump is reaching out to Russia to end its war against the country.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: European troops, US support are part of an emerging plan for Ukraine. But it faces many hurdles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As the U.S. and Russia push ahead — so far without Ukraine at the table – on talks to end the war, political and military leaders in Europe are fleshing out details of a plan for European forces to help ensure Moscow doesn't attack again.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China issued 'disconcerting' warning of live-fire exercises to planes flying above, Australia says
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Airliners were over the Tasman Sea crossing between Australia and New Zealand when the Chinese navy warned they were flying over a secret live-fire exercise, Australian Defense Minister Richard Marles said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-MCLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid freeze puts HIV-positive orphans in Kenya at risk as medical supplies dwindle
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Two-year-old Evans was brought to the Nyumbani Children’s Home in Nairobi, Kenya a year ago, suffering from HIV and tuberculosis. With no family to care for him, Evans was referred to the orphanage by a health center after he stopped responding to medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS KOMU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A news conference between Zelenskyy and Trump’s Ukraine envoy is canceled as tension rise
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A news conference that was planned to follow talks between Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Remains of young mom Shiri Bibas, taken hostage and killed by Hamas, not returned despite promise, Israel says
item.description: The mother of a woman abducted with her two children and husband was not among the slain hostages returned to Israel by Hamas on Thursday, Israel said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Terror attack rocks Israel; three buses reportedly explode
item.description: Israel officials have ordered all bus and train services halted while all vehicles are inspected for bombs following two bus explosions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fearing Trump will cross border militarily to pursue cartels, Mexico moves to reform constitution
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said she will propose constitutional reforms aimed at protecting the country’s sovereignty over concerns that the U.S. military could pursue drug cartels inside Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia's invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MIKE PESOLI and STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Delta plane crash: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: At least 18 people dead after stampede at New Delhi railway station
item.description: “The crowd went out of control and no one could control it,” said Nikhil Kumar, a shopkeeper who witnessed the crowd surge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
